meaning: n.搜集并揭发丑事的人(尤指新闻记者)
examples: That journalist is nothing but a professional muckraker.
Having discovered his vocation as a cultural muckraker, he eventually secured an audience, a market and even the offer (which was refused) of Westminster Abbey as a final resting place.
The equipment leasing industry has never faced a muckraker quite like Rhonda Roland Shearer.
Worth-based WFAA News 8 on-air reporter Byron Harris, a 38-year veteran multiple-award winning muckraker.
This derivative, flimsy black-comic muckraker, set in a V.
